message:
fix(lua,store): nested table assignment write-back and deterministic set ordering

Lua evaluator (fr-command/lua_eval.rs):
- Fix nested table field/index assignment (e.g., t.a.b = 42 or
  t[1][2] = 99) which previously only mutated a clone without
  propagating changes back up the parent chain. Add
  write_back_table_expr() that recursively walks the expression tree
  (Name → Index → Field) to write the modified table value back
  through each level of nesting.
- Thread varargs parameter through table_set_by_expr() and the new
  write_back method so nested expressions can be evaluated correctly
  in variadic function contexts.
- Add two regression tests proving nested field and index assignment
  now persist correctly.

Store (fr-store/lib.rs):
- Replace HashSet<Vec<u8>> with BTreeSet<Vec<u8>> for Redis Set values
  throughout the store. This makes SMEMBERS, SSCAN, SUNIONSTORE,
  SINTERSTORE, SDIFFSTORE, and RDB deserialization produce
  deterministic iteration order, eliminating non-determinism in
  command outputs that depend on set enumeration order (SMEMBERS,
  SSCAN cursor advancement, SRANDMEMBER with negative count).
- Remove now-unnecessary sort() calls in smembers() and sscan() since
  BTreeSet iterates in sorted order by definition.
- Simplify sscan() to use iterator skip+take pattern instead of
  index-based access.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
